column27: Cho's rare was awesome in doing big damage to the masterminds and villains. Didn't see that card on the second round sadly.

I liked how the scheme twist was basically a free turn. Was sitting on a full city most of the time on game 2 so twists helped a bunch. 2nd hand just kept clearing the henchmen while hand 1 was going full combo no strikes hit (actually was going to have 2 on the next 2 turns.
Drax and Star Lord were the All Stars. Drax rare (Avatar of Destruction) was played 3 times in the game, and in one occasion generated 48 attack!!!

Guess which side had Sentient Starship and Godslayer Blade, and a handful of other artifacts. Not to mention 4 copies Legendary Outlaw. Regularly getting over 20 attack was great, and the final turn was more than 40 after getting set up by Force of Eternity.  Game two was looking pretty good until Morg showed up and buried all the cards I was building for, ultimately it was slower. 

Gamora was my first pick because I wanted more shards and to ignore the shards Magus and  the kree could stack up in the city. I went back and forth on pick 2 seriously considering Groot or someone with Feast.

I chose Bishop and Warpath to try and pair with the other heroes. I didn't think this would be a hard set up but I wasn't sure how well they would mash up. I didn't want to change heroes for the 2nd playthrough and it went pretty terrible so I got lucky on playthrough 1.

I had either a great (or terrible) shuffle and had 3 strikes and 1 twist hit in the first 4 cards, so that helped me get set-up without loosing anything for my first few turns. I was able to get Shatterstar's rare by turn 10 and clone it so I focused on KO and draw power so I could start hitting Apocalypse. I could have finished the game earlier but I twice pulled Apocalypse's Immortal Tactic after shattering him and ended up having to fight him 7 times.

Went all in on Ranged, which worked first attempt but wasn't fast enough on the second try. I had one hand Ranged/Instinct and the other Ranged/Strength. Rictor and Shatterstar are obviously great together, but Rictor better watch out because Thor might steal his boyfriend. Asgard Thor's focus on Worthy worked perfectly with Shatterstar's 5 cost focus.

I'm certain the only way you win this setup is by getting unbelievably lucky. Assuming the City is empty, any Villain who enters the City on a Twist is worth at least 6 Attack. Any of them. So you're automatically going to fail the last two Twists that come up. The only way you win this game is by getting insanely lucky with the first 7 Twists, and you can't even rush Apocalypse since keeping the City clear avoids the loss condition. This is why I hate fighting Apocalypse. Not even because of him, but the Four Horsemen get an insane buff beyond already-debilitating Fight effects. Easily my least favourite Mastermind in the game, I have no idea why he isn't worth 7VP.

The attack bonuses on the Four Horsemen and Dark Avengers make it really tough to avoid the Guardians being defeated by the Scheme. My decks starting hitting a bit of a stride near the end, but I was already on the edge of defeat. With a cleared city, any Villain that re-entered was going to defeat my final Guardian.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.

column27: Apocalypse combined with sentinel ONE and beefy dark avengers!? POW! BAM! BOOM!….TO MY FACE! lol. We just couldn’t get a handle over these tough villains in the city and lost to the scheme. Cool and cruel scenario. If you want a spanking, play this. :)
Thought I was good with Iceman, but he just moved way too slow.
villains were way too strong and defeated the guardian every time.

column2: Red Skull Floods the Planet with Melted Glaciers
column27: Felt that speed would be key on this one as high VP would be offset by too many turns. Not sure if that turned out to be the case. At 13 turns didn't get much of a chance to optimize or get the engine going, just a couple big hits to wrap it up.
Fun setup just bad luck on our hands early not coming together
